title: ds-viz-image - option matrix (light)
description: Test bench for splunk.image. Verifies all options from the 10.4 PDF: src (string URL, all browser-supported formats), preserveAspectRatio (boolean). Demonstrates URL reference vs uploaded images, aspect-ratio modes, layering (image-as-background, image-as-watermark), sizing patterns, and the absolute-layout-only constraint.

options.markdown:
# `splunk.image` - option matrix

Images are an absolute-layout-only feature. Use them for **logos**, **diagrams**, **background canvases**, **watermarks**, and **icon overlays**.

Full options list from the **10.4 PDF**:
- `src` (URL of an image; **png / jpeg / gif / webp / svg** supported)
- `preserveAspectRatio` (default `false` - the image stretches to fill the panel)

Two ways to source an image:
1. **Uploaded to KV store** (Enterprise admins, Cloud `sc_admins`, power users) - rendered in PDF/PNG exports.
2. **Referenced by URL** - any user, but **NOT** included in PDF/PNG exports and the URL domain must be in the **Dashboards Trusted Domains List** (see panel below).

viz_trust_warning.title: Read this first - Dashboards Trusted Domains List

options.markdown:
## Why some image panels below may show a placeholder icon

Splunk now blocks **any external image URL** whose domain is not on the per-instance **Dashboards Trusted Domains List**. If a panel shows the broken-image placeholder, the browser console will log:

> *External image URLs must now have their domains listed in the Dashboards Trusted Domains List by working with your administrator. Alternatively, you can upload the image directly into the dashboard.*

### To make external panels render

Ask your Splunk admin to add the domain to:

```
$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/system/local/web.conf
[settings]
dashboards_image_allow_list = www.splunk.com, splunk.com
```

Then restart Splunkweb. Splunk-bundled `/en-US/static/...` paths (panels 1, 2, 5, 8, 12, 13 below) are **always** trusted and never need this step.

### What this bench uses

External panels point at `splunk-white-black-bg.png` and `logo-splunk-corp-rgb-k-web.svg` on `www.splunk.com`. Both are real, public assets. If they 404 in your instance, the trust list is the cause - not the URL.

viz_default.title: 1. Default (preserveAspectRatio=false)
viz_default.description: Image stretches to fill the panel - the PDF default. Rarely what you want for logos or photos.

viz_aspect_true.title: 2. preserveAspectRatio=true
viz_aspect_true.description: Image is letterboxed inside the panel - intended ratio is preserved. Use this for logos and photos.

viz_logo_square.title: 3. Logo in a square panel
viz_logo_square.description: Square panel + preserveAspectRatio=true => logo centers cleanly with empty space on the longer axis.

viz_logo_wide.title: 4. Logo in a wide panel
viz_logo_wide.description: Wide panel + preserveAspectRatio=true => same logo, different aspect ratio. Demonstrates that the panel size, not the option, drives the visual.

viz_local_path.title: 5. Local Splunk-bundled asset
viz_local_path.description: Bundled `/en-US/static/...` paths are always allow-listed and render in PDF/PNG. Great for placeholders.

viz_external_cdn.title: 6. External CDN URL (PNG)
viz_external_cdn.description: External URLs require the domain to be on the Dashboards Trusted Domains List. External images do NOT render in PDF/PNG export.

viz_svg.title: 7. SVG image
viz_svg.description: Browsers render SVG natively, so `splunk.image` accepts it. Resolution-independent - perfect for logos, icons, diagrams.

viz_background_layer.title: 8. Image as background layer
viz_background_layer.description: Stretched image used as a section backdrop. Layer KPIs on top via z-order.

viz_overlay_kpi.title: Throughput
viz_overlay_kpi.description: Floats on top of viz_background_layer at the same coordinates - earlier in `structure` array means lower z-order.

viz_watermark.title: 9. Faint watermark layer
viz_watermark.description: Logo with low opacity - achieved by uploading a pre-faded PNG/SVG. `splunk.image` itself has no opacity option, so you must bake it into the asset.

viz_no_src.title: 10. Empty src (fallback panel)
viz_no_src.description: When `src` is missing or fails to load, the panel shows the placeholder image. Use a markdown fallback panel instead, like this one.

options.markdown:
**Image not configured.** Set `options.src` to a KV-store-uploaded image or an allow-listed URL.

Supported formats:
- `png`
- `jpeg`
- `gif`
- `webp`
- `svg` (via URL reference)

viz_diagram.title: 11. SVG (architecture diagram pattern)
viz_diagram.description: SVG architecture diagrams render crisply at any panel size. Use SVG over PNG for diagrams. Stand-in here is the Splunk corporate logo SVG.

viz_floor_plan.title: 12. Floor plan / facility map
viz_floor_plan.description: Datacenter background image - layer rectangle 'rooms' or singleValue 'sensors' on top using absolute coordinates.
